meaning: v.加快,鼓舞,刺激,生长,加速
examples: She felt her heartbeat quicken as he approached.
As the pace of modern life continues to quicken, the pressure seems to be on the increase all the time.
We have to quicken our pace.
For a given Quicken query we generally used the default choice, unless it seemed clearly wrong.
Good intentions, and a range of strong performances, are never quite enough to quicken the pulse.
